analysis: The current corpus heavily exercises the API mode with non‑templated input directories, yielding many WARNINGs, but it barely touches the prompt conversion logic, the VCS/ZIP handling, and the deep‑merge code in generate.py. Hook scripts, overwrite/skip flags, and replay payloads are under‑used. Consequently, large blocks of generate.py, prompt.py, zipfile.py, vcs.py and config.py remain uncovered.

line_target_hints.code_signal: assigning a new key into a nested context dictionary when a variable is a dictionary variable
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Add an alternative to extra_context_object that yields a nested dictionary structure (e.g., '{ "project_name": { "sub": "value" } }').
line_target_hints.required_inputs: extra_context_object must contain a nested dict, e.g. { "project_name": { "sub": "value" } } and invocation_object.no_input = false so the generator walks the dict.

line_target_hints.code_signal: handling list values inside a dictionary variable during deep‑merge
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Extend extra_context_object with a case that includes a list literal (JSON array) for a key.
line_target_hints.required_inputs: extra_context_object with a list value for a known variable, e.g. { "license": ["MIT", "GPL"] } and a matching variable in cookiecutter_json_object.

line_target_hints.code_signal: conversion of user input strings to boolean choices (yes/no)
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Add a new rule extra_context_object_bool that can emit boolean‑style strings ("yes", "no", "y", "n", "true", "false") for boolean prompts.
line_target_hints.required_inputs: invocation_object.no_input = false and prompts_object must contain a boolean‑type prompt (e.g., "use_docker"), then extra_context_object supplies values like "yes", "no", "y", "n".

line_target_hints.code_signal: expanding a user‑provided clone_to_dir path with Path.expanduser
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Introduce a top‑level optional field (e.g., "zip_clone_dir") in the case manifest and map it to the harness call.
line_target_hints.required_inputs: A manifest that sets a top‑level field (e.g., "zip_clone_dir": "~/tmp/template") which the harness passes to zipfile.clone_to_dir(). The grammar does not currently expose such a field.

line_target_hints.code_signal: splitting a repo URL of the form "git+https://example.com/repo.git"
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Add a rule repo_url_literal that can produce strings with a '+' separator (git+, hg+).
line_target_hints.required_inputs: A manifest that includes a field "repo_url": "git+https://example.com/repo.git" passed to the harness when invoking the template source loader.

line_target_hints.code_signal: os.path.expandvars and expanduser on a path containing $HOME or ~
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Add a rule config_path_expr that can embed '~' or '${HOME}' placeholders.
line_target_hints.required_inputs: A manifest field "config_path": "~/{{cookiecutter.repo_name}}"" with an environment variable set (e.g., $HOME).

line_target_hints.code_signal: deep copy of default configuration and merging overrides
line_target_hints.grammar_implication: Add an extra_context variant that includes a nested dict named "default_context" to trigger the deep‑copy/merge path.
line_target_hints.required_inputs: extra_context_object that supplies a top‑level key matching a config option (e.g., "default_context": { "project_slug": "myproj" })

reachable_by_harness_only: zipfile.clone_to_dir path expansion (requires a real filesystem path)
vcs.repo_url parsing (needs a URL string supplied by the harness)
config.expand_path handling of environment variables and ~ (requires harness to set env vars)
generate.py deep‑merge of dictionary variables that are only created when extra_context contains nested dicts not expressed in the current grammar

recommended_rule_edits.rationale: Current alternatives only provide flat string values. Adding a nested dictionary alternative and a list‑value alternative will allow the generator's deep‑merge logic (lines 72‑78) to be exercised.
Introduce a boolean‑style string alternative ("yes", "no", "y", "n") so that prompt._convert_to_bool() (prompt.py lines 53‑58) is exercised when no_input=false.
Add a fourth alternative where one of the hook scripts raises an exception after a successful pre_prompt, to hit failure paths in cookiecutter/hooks.py.
Create a third alternative that mixes overwrite_if_exists=true with skip_if_file_exists=false (and vice‑versa) to explore all flag combinations in generate.py.
Add a single‑element list alternative (one nested_template_entry) to trigger the loop that processes nested templates without requiring a second entry.
Add an explicit '"\r"' alternative to increase line‑break handling coverage.
New rule (top‑level) to emit repo URLs with a '+' separator, enabling vcs.py parsing.
New rule (top‑level) to emit paths containing '~' or '${HOME}' so config.expand_path is exercised.

underexplored_scenario_families: Successful and failing hook scripts (pre_gen_project, post_gen_project)
Binary file handling with copy_without_render patterns
CLI mode with no_input=false and default‑driven answers
Path expansion via config.expand_path and zipfile.clone_to_dir handling
unreachable_harness_limits: The harness cannot create actual files on disk, so binary file write‑out paths cannot be verified beyond grammar generation.
Network‑based VCS cloning cannot be performed in the sandbox, limiting coverage of vcs.clone_repo.
